tags.description: Manage data quality of metadata resources
tags.name: data quality

tags.description: This API is in active development. Its definition may change frequently and without notice.
tags.name: experimental

post.description: Cancel a running query.

Test a virtual connection. For increased security, connection endpoints require an Enterprise Admin
Token. This token can be found under [Advanced Settings](https://data.world/settings/advanced).

401.description: User is unauthorized to perform action

422.description: Unable to parse request. URLs provided must be fully-formed http or https URLs

get.description:
Get a list of configured virtual connections by owner. For increased security, connection
endpoints require an Enterprise Admin Token. This token can be found under
[Advanced Settings](https://data.world/settings/advanced).

post.description:
Create a new virtual connection. For increased security, connection endpoints require an Enterprise Admin
Token. This token can be found under [Advanced Settings](https://data.world/settings/advanced).

parameters.description: Validate connection before save
parameters.name: validate

delete.description:
Delete a Connection. For increased security, connection endpoints require an Enterprise Admin Token. This
token can be found under [Advanced Settings](https://data.world/settings/advanced).

get.description:
Get details for an individual virtual connection. For increased security, connection endpoints require an
Enterprise Admin Token. This token can be found under
[Advanced Settings](https://data.world/settings/advanced).

Shows audits that happened during processing of data quality inputs.
One of "requestId" or "resourceIri" needs to be provided.
Only errors are tracked. Audits expire 1 day after creation. Returns a maximum of 100 audits.
If there are no audits, it is likely the request was executed successfully.

Access requirement: Requires an admin token. It also requires admin access to the organization.

This endpoint is in active development. Its definition and behavior may change frequently and without notice.

parameters.description: organization agent ID
parameters.name: orgAgentId

parameters.description: Internationalized Resource Identifier (IRI) of the resource. You can find the IRI in the Technical Reference section under the Settings tab on the resource’s catalog page.
parameters.name: resourceIri

401.description: User is unauthorized to perform action

post.description:
Associate a metadata resource with a Badge that describes its overall data quality status.

Access requirement: Requires an admin token. It also requires admin access to the organization.

This endpoint is in active development. Its definition and behavior may change frequently and without notice.

parameters.description: organization agent ID
parameters.name: orgAgentId

401.description: User is unauthorized to perform action

post.description:
Add executions of a check against a metadata resource.

Access requirement: Requires an admin token. It also requires admin access to the organization.

This endpoint is in active development. Its definition and behavior may change frequently and without notice.

parameters.description: organization agent ID
parameters.name: orgAgentId

parameters.description: The collection to insert resources in. Identified by IRI. You can find the IRI in the Technical Reference section under the Settings tab on the resource’s catalog page.
parameters.name: collectionIri

401.description: User is unauthorized to perform action

post.description:
Asynchronously delete data quality Checks and all associated Check Versions and Check Runs.

This operation enqueues delete requests for background processing. The response indicates
how many requests were successfully enqueued, not how many deletions have completed.

Response fields:
- success: Number of delete requests successfully enqueued
- failed: Number of delete requests that failed to enqueue
- requestId: Correlation ID for tracking

Access requirement: Requires an admin token. It also requires admin access to the organization.

This endpoint is in active development. Its definition and behavior may change frequently and without notice.

parameters.description: organization agent ID
parameters.name: orgAgentId

401.description: User is unauthorized to perform action

get.description:
List datasets that the currently authenticated user has access to, for the specified owner; when the
dataset is open, private (but discoverable by the authenticated user) or the authenticated user is a
contributor with discover visibility

parameters.description: Number of results to return
parameters.name: limit

parameters.description: A token supplied by the previous response to retrieve the next page of results
parameters.name: next

get.summary: List datasets for a specified owner

post.description: Create a new dataset.

delete.description:
Delete a dataset and associated data. This operation cannot be undone, but you may recreate the dataset
using the same id.

get.description:
Retrieve a dataset. The definition of the dataset will be returned, not its data. Use
`GET:/download/{owner}/{id}` or `GET:/file_download/{owner}/{id}/{file}` to retrieve the original files
content, or `POST:/sql/{owner}/{id}` or `POST:/sparql/{owner}/{id}` to query the data.

patch.description:
Update an existing dataset. Only elements included in the request will be updated. All omitted elements
will remain untouched. Requesting change in a list replaces the entire list. Requesting an element to be
null deletes all values on the element.

put.description:
Create or replace a dataset with a given id. If a dataset exists with the same id, this call will reset
such dataset and all the data contained in it.

delete.description: Delete a DOI ([Digital Object Identifier](https://www.doi.org/)) associated with a dataset.

put.description: Associate a DOI ([Digital Object Identifier](https://www.doi.org/)) with a dataset.

delete.description:
Delete one or more files from a dataset.

**Batching**
Note that the `name` parameter can be include multiple times in the query string, once for each file that is to be deleted together in a single request.

post.description:
Add files from URLs to a dataset. This method allows files published on the web to be added to a
data.world dataset via their URL. This method can also be used to retrieve data via web APIs, with
advanced options for http method, request payload and authentication.

The source URL will be stored so you can easily update your file anytime it changes via the *fetch latest*
link on the [data.world](https://data.world/) dataset page or by triggering the `GET:/sync` endpoint.

delete.description: Delete a single file from a dataset.

parameters.description: Filename
parameters.name: file

get.description: Get file description and labels

patch.description:
Update file description and labels. Only elements included in the request will be updated. All omitted
elements will remain untouched. Requesting change in a list replaces the entire list. Requesting an element
to be null deletes all values on the element.

put.description: Replace file description and labels

get.description:
List published saved queries associated with a dataset. Query definitions will be returned, not the query results.
To retrieve query results use `GET:/queries/{id}/results`.

parameters.description: Number of results to return
parameters.name: limit

parameters.description: A token supplied by the previous response to retrieve the next page of results
parameters.name: next

post.description: Create a saved query in a specified dataset.
post.summary: Create a saved query in a specified dataset

delete.description: Delete a saved query in a specified dataset.
delete.summary: Delete a saved query in a specified dataset

put.description: Update a saved query in a specified dataset.
put.summary: Update a saved query in a specified dataset

get.description: Same as `POST:/dataset/{owner}/{id}/sync`. The id parameter can either be a dataset id or project id.

post.description:
Sync files within a dataset or project. This method will process the latest data available for files added
from URLs or via streams. The id parameter can either be a dataset id or project id.

get.summary: Fetch latest file from source and update dataset — via GET, for convenience.

post.summary: Fetch latest file from source and update dataset.

post.description:
Add tables from an established virtual connection. For increased security, endpoints that interact with
external connection sources require an Enterprise Admin Token. This token can be found under
[Advanced Settings](https://data.world/settings/advanced). To learn more about the virtual connections
data.world supports, please visit our
[help portal](https://help.data.world/hc/en-us/sections/360009504254-Create-and-manage-virtual-connections).
post.summary: Add live tables from a virtual connection

patch.description: Delete properties from a maximum of 25 resources of any type in the organization at once. To delete properties from a single resource, use "Delete properties from a resource".

For example, in the URL: https://acme.data.world/jonloyens/an-intro-to-dataworld-dataset, jonloyens is the unique identifier of the owner.
parameters.name: owner

For example, in the URL: https://acme.data.world/jonloyens/an-intro-to-dataworld-dataset, jonloyens is the unique identifier of the owner.
parameters.name: owner

patch.description: Update a resource of any type in the organization. Properties that are not defined in the request are not updated. When updating properties that have a single value, the old value is replaced by the new value in the request. When updating properties that have a list of values, see "updateListBy" query parameter for more granular controls. To delete properties, use "Delete properties from a resource". To update multiple resources at once, use "Bulk: update multiple resources at once".

parameters.description:
User name and unique identifier of the user or organization a resource belongs to.
For example, in the URL: https://acme.data.world/jonloyens/an-intro-to-dataworld-dataset, jonloyens is the unique identifier of the owner.
parameters.name: owner

parameters.description: Internationalized Resource Identifier (IRI) of the resource. You can find the IRI in the Technical Reference section under the Settings tab on the resource’s catalog page.
parameters.name: iri

parameters.description: Set this option when updating properties that supports having a list of values. Given a list of values in the request body, should this request add to the existing list, remove the specified values, or replace the existing list with the specified values?
parameters.name: updateListBy

post.description: Create a new resource of any type in the organization. The resource is created with the provided properties. To create multiple resources at once, use "Bulk: create multiple resources at once".

parameters.description:
User name and unique identifier of the user or organization a resource belongs to.
For example, in the URL: https://acme.data.world/jonloyens/an-intro-to-dataworld-dataset, jonloyens is the unique identifier of the owner.
parameters.name: owner

post.description: This is an advanced operation and should be used with caution. The data.world catalog graph relies on a layered model that keeps the content from collected source systems separate from edits made by end users on the platform. This allows the collected source system data to update regularly withoutoverwriting enrichment and curation efforts that are authored on data.world. Sometimes, however, it is desirable to overwrite the end user edits or let the source system metadata replace user edits. This endpoint can be used to clear all end user edits for a given metadata resource or list of resources, restoring the source system values.
post.summary: Clear all user edits from a resource

put.description: This is an advanced operation and should be used with caution. The data.world catalog graph relies on a layered model that keeps the content from collected source systems separate from edits made by end users on the platform. This allows the collected source system data to update regularly without overwriting enrichment and curation efforts that are authored on data.world. Sometimes, however, it is desirable to overwrite the end user edits or let the source system metadata replace user edits. This endpoint can be used to clear a specific user-edited metadata field, restoring the source system values.
put.summary: Clear all user edits on specified properties from a resource

List projects that the currently authenticated user has access to, for the specified owner; when the
project is open, or when project is private but has view/edit/manage permissions for the authenticated user

get.summary: List projects for a specified owner

post.description: Create a new project.

delete.description:
Delete a project and associated data. This operation cannot be undone, but you may recreate the project
using the same id.

get.description:
Retrieve a project. The definition of the project will be returned, not the associated data. Use
`POST:/sql/{owner}/{id}` or `POST:/sparql/{owner}/{id}` to query the data or use dataset APIs to retrieve
data from linked datasets.

patch.description:
Update an existing project. Only elements included in the request will be updated. All omitted elements
will remain untouched. Requesting change in a list replaces the entire list. Requesting an element to be
null deletes all values on the element.

put.description:
Create or replace a project with a given id. If a project exists with the same id, this call will reset
such project redefining all its attributes.

delete.description: Remove a linked dataset from a project.

put.description: Add a linked dataset to a project.

get.description: Retrieve a project version. The definition of the project will be returned.

Get supported metadata properties by owner.

This endpoint can be used to obtain a reference list of supported custom metadata properties for your
organization's catalog. Custom properties for resources in your catalog require an ApiBinding to be
configured for use in the public API. For more information about how to correctly configure ApiBindings,
please reach out to your data.world support representative or message help@data.world.
get.summary: Get supported metadata properties by owner

get.description:
Retrieve a saved query. Query definitions will be returned, not query results. To retrieve query results
use `GET:/queries/{id}/results`.

Execute a saved query.

SQL results are available in a variety of formats. By default, `application/json` will be returned. Set
the `Accept` header to one of the following values in accordance with your preference:

* `text/csv`
* `application/json`
* `application/json-l`
* `application/x-ndjson`

SPARQL results are available in a variety of formats. By default, `application/sparql-results+json` will
be returned. Set the `Accept` header to one of the following values in accordance with your preference:

- `application/sparql-results+xml`
- `application/sparql-results+json`
- `application/rdf+json`
- `application/rdf+xml`
- `text/csv`
- `text/tab-separated-values`
- `text/turtle`

parameters.description: Query token to use with cancel
parameters.name: queryruntoken

post.description:
Execute a saved query (same as GET:/queries/{id}/results) with the option to specify named query
parameters.

parameters.description: Query token to use with cancel
parameters.name: queryruntoken

get.description: Retrieve a version of a saved query. Query definitions will be returned, not query results.

post.description: Accept a resource request. The defined contribution or transfer will happen. For more details on resource requests, see "Get a resource request".

Create a request asking for access against a dataset. A request can ask the resource admin for permission to access a resource. It may also be an invitation from the resource admin to give others access to a resource.
Accept an authorization request. This will give the grantee access to the resource. For more details about authorization request, see "Create an authorization request".

get.description: Get a list of authorization requests that can be approved or rejected by the user. For more details about authorization request, see "Create an authorization request".

get.summary: Get authorization requests that can be approved/rejected

post.description: Delete an authorization request. This will delete the request. The grantee will not receive any access to the resource. For more details about authorization request, see "Create an authorization request".

Reject an authorization request. This will delete the request. The grantee will not receive any access to the resource. For more details about authorization request, see "Create an authorization request".

post.description:
Simple search is a light weight API implementation exposing similar search capabilities as our main
platform search UI.

By default, the search response will return mixed result types including metadata resources, datasets,
projects, queries, insights, tables, and comments.

A free-text search term is required. Simple search does not support complex keyword or logical operator
grammar.  Light weight filters for resource owner and resource category are supported. You may optionally
specify the includeCommunityResults param to query for resources in the data.world open data community.

get.summary: Gets all service accounts by owner.

get.description:
Execute a SPARQL query. The query will default to using the :current graph if the query contains no FROM
clauses and no default graph URI parameters are specified, in which case it will return a 404 if the
:current graph does not exist for the org.

SPARQL results are available in a variety of formats. By default, `application/sparql-results+json` will
be returned. Set the `Accept` header to one of the following values in accordance with your preference
and the type of the query (SELECT, CONSTRUCT, DESCRIBE, ASK):

- `application/sparql-results+xml`
- `application/sparql-results+json`
- `application/rdf+json`
- `application/rdf+xml`
- `text/csv`
- `text/tab-separated-values`
- `text/turtle`

New to SPARQL? Check out data.world's [SPARQL tutorial](https://docs.data.world/tutorials/sparql/).

parameters.description: Specify URI for named graph(s) to query, overriding any FROM clauses in the query. If the default-graph-uri parameter is omitted and there are no FROM clauses, the :current graph will be used. See [Our Docs](https://docs.data.world/en/168180-understanding-the-catalog-graph.html#UUID-46e644b5-119a-a4a2-eb69-e09ce33bd986) for more info.
parameters.name: default-graph-uri

parameters.description: Specify URI for named graph(s) to query, overriding any FROM NAMED clauses in the query. See [Our Docs](https://docs.data.world/en/168180-understanding-the-catalog-graph.html#UUID-46e644b5-119a-a4a2-eb69-e09ce33bd986) for more info.
parameters.name: named-graph-uri

parameters.description: Query token to use with cancel
parameters.name: queryruntoken

get.summary: Execute SPARQL query via GET with the default graph

post.description:
Execute a SQL query against a dataset or data project.

SQL results are available in a variety of formats. By default, `application/json` will be returned. Set
the `Accept` header to one of the following values in accordance with your preference:

* `text/csv`
* `application/json`
* `application/json-l`
* `application/x-ndjson`

New to SQL? Check out data.world's [SQL manual](https://docs.data.world/tutorials/dwsql/) .

This endpoint describes what columns will be included if the given SQL query is performed against the specified dataset.
New to SQL? Check out data.world's [SQL manual](https://docs.data.world/tutorials/dwsql/) .

Append JSON data to a stream associated with a dataset.

data.world streams are append-only by default. Alternatively, if a primary key is specified (see:
`POST:/streams/{owner}/{id}/{streamId}/schema`), data.world will replace records with the same primary
key value.

**Streams don't need to be created before you can append data to them**. They will be created on-demand,
 when the first record is appended or by defining its schema.

Multiple records can be appended at once by using JSON-L (`application/json-l`) as the request content type.

**IMPORTANT**

Data uploaded to a dataset via a stream is not immediatelly processed. Instead, it is processed
automatically in accordance with the dataset settings (default: daily) or as a result of calling
`POST:/datasets/{owner}/{id}/sync`.

Once processed, the contents of a stream will appear as part of the respective dataset as a `.jsonl`
file (e.g. `my-stream` will produce a file named `my-stream.jsonl`).

delete.description: Delete all records previously appended to stream.

get.description: Retrieve stream schema.

patch.description:
"Set or update a stream's schema.

The schema of a stream defines its primary key(s) and sort/sequence field.

data.world streams are append-only by default. Alternatively, if a primary key is specified, data.world
will replace records with the same primary key value. data.world will sort records by sequence field
value and will discard all but the last record appended for each given primary key value.

The `updateMethod` parameter specifies how data.world should handle existing records when schema is
updated. Currently, the only `updateMethod` supported is `TRUNCATED`. data.world will discard all
records when the schema is updated."

get.description: This API retrieves recently updated comments that are open and discoverable. Users can filter results by the owner's ID and specify the number of comments to fetch, with a limit of 100 and a default of 5.

parameters.name: owner

parameters.description: Optional parameter to return number of matched comments with limit of 100 and default size of 5
parameters.name: size

get.description: This API allows users to access trending topics determined by their level of activity. Users can specify the number of topics to retrieve per request, with a maximum limit of 1000 topics. If the number is not specified, it defaults to 10.

parameters.description: Optional parameter to return number of found hot topics with limit of 100 and default size of 5
parameters.name: size

get.description: Retrieve open and discoverable topics associated with a specific owner. If an optional resource is provided in the query parameter, this will retrieve topics for the resource that is owned by the owner. To reference a dataset insight, both `datasetId` and `insightUuid` should be provided. To reference a metadata resource, `iri` should be provided."

delete.description: This API allows users to remove a topic they have permission to access, specified by the owner's ID and the topic's UUID. This DELETE request ensures the topic is successfully deleted if access criteria are met.

get.description: Access an individual topic using this API by providing the owner's identifier and the unique topic UUID. This API ensures user permissions are validated for visibility and access to the specified topic.

put.description: This API enables users to modify details of a topic they have access to, identified by the owner's ID and the topic's UUID. This PUT request ensures that all changes comply with the required access permissions and topic visibility settings.

get.description: This API allows users to retrieve comments for a specific topic they have access to, identified by the owner's ID and the topic's UUID. Users can control the number of comments returned, with options to sort and paginate results.

parameters.description: Size of page to fetch
parameters.name: size

parameters.description: Result number to start fetch from (not the page number to start from)
parameters.name: from

parameters.description: Sort results by fields
parameters.name: sort

post.description: This API enables users to add a comment to a specific topic, provided they have access permissions. This POST request requires the owner's ID and the topic's UUID, along with the comment content, to successfully create and store the comment.

delete.description: This API allows users to remove a comment from a topic, provided they have access rights. This DELETE request requires the owner's ID, topic's UUID, and the comment ID to successfully execute the deletion.

get.description: This API allows users to access a specific comment within a topic, provided they have the necessary access permissions. This GET request requires the owner's ID and the topic's UUID to retrieve the comment details.

put.description: This API allows users to modify the content of a comment within a topic, provided they have the necessary permissions. By using a PUT request with the owner's ID, topic's UUID, and comment ID, users can update the comment's details effectively.

post.description:
"Upload multiple files at once to a dataset.

This endpoint expects requests of type `multipart/form-data` and you can include one or more parts named
`file`, each containing a different file to be uploaded.

For example, assuming that you want to upload two local files named `file1.csv` and `file2.csv` to a
hypothetical dataset `https://data.world/awesome-user/awesome-dataset`, this is what the cURL command
would look like.

```bash
curl \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer <YOUR_API_TOKEN>" \
  -F "file=@file1.csv" \
  -F "file=@file2.csv" \
  https://api.data.world/v0/uploads/awesome-user/awesome-dataset/files
```

Swagger clients may limit this method of upload to one file at a time. Other HTTP clients capable of
making multipart/form-data requests can be used to upload multiple files in a single request."

put.description:
Upload one file at a time to a dataset.

This endpoint expects requests of type `application/octet-stream`.

For example, assuming that you want to upload a local file named `file1.csv` to a hypothetical dataset
`https://data.world/awesome-user/awesome-dataset` and choose its name on data.world to be
`better-name.csv`, this is what the cURL command would look like.

```bash
curl \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer <YOUR_API_TOKEN>" \
  -X PUT -H "Content-Type: application/octet-stream" \
  --data-binary @file1.csv \
  https://api.data.world/v0/uploads/awesome-user/awesome-dataset/files/better-name.csv
```

This method of upload is typically not supported by Swagger clients. Other HTTP clients can be used to
supply the contents of the file directly in the body of the request.

context.description:
The original organization agent ID that the resource was collected into. This can be found in the RDF
file outputted by the collector. In the RDF, there is a resource of type "dwec:CatalogCuration" containing
many configuration properties. The "context" property is this field.

databaseLocation.description:
Database server host and port, formatted as "host:port". This can be found in the RDF file outputted by the
collector. In the RDF, there is a resource of type "dwec:CatalogCuration" containing many configuration
properties. The "host" property is part of this field. Port can be found under any "dwec:Database"
resource under the predicate "dwec:databasePort".

similarResources.description: Similar datasets with the same owner

DeleteCheckDto.description: A data quality check to delete

checkId.description: Check identifier as provided by the data quality provider

source.description: Data quality provider name as given at resource creation

DeleteChecksDto.description: A list of data quality checks to delete

checks.description: The list of checks to delete

iri.description: Internationalized Resource Identifier (IRI) of the resource. You can find the IRI in the Technical Reference section under the Settings tab on the resource’s catalog page.

IRIResource.description: A metadata resource represented by an IRI

dataSourceLinks.description: Data source links included in a PATCH request will replace existing data source links.

collections.description: Catalog Collection to which this metadata resource is added. Required for POST and PUT.Available catalog collection can be discovered via appropriate discovery endpoints.

items.description: Catalog Collection to which this metadata resource is added. Required for POST and PUT.Available catalog collection can be discovered via appropriate discovery endpoints.

description.description: A short, but descriptive statement about the metadata resource.
description.example: Aid given to charity is an option that can be exercised by individuals and corporations

properties.description: Custom properties for the metadata resource mapped to API Bindings. Can be simple name-value string pairs or nested values for a string name. See examples for details.

tags.description: A collection of tags to identify the relevance of metadata resource. Tags with no spaces is defacto standard

items.description: A collection of tags to identify the relevance of metadata resource. Tags with no spaces is defacto standard

title.description: Title of the metadata resource. Required for POST and PUT.

typeLabel.description: Indicates the type of metadata resource. Some examples of valid values are Report, Tableau dashboard, Glossary, Table, Database view etc. Default values will be assumed if not provided. Defaults to Report for analysis resources, Glossary for Glossary resources, Table for Table resources and Column for Column resources. Once specified during creation, this cannot be changed via PATCH. Only a PUT can change the type

typeLabel.description: Indicates the type of metadata resource. Some examples of valid values are Report, Tableau dashboard, Glossary, Table, Database view etc. Default values will be assumed if not provided. avatarUrl.description: URL that points to a small, graphical representation of an organization

createdRange.description: Filter by range of date that the resource was created.

minAccessLevel.description: Minimum access level to filter by.

owner.description:
User name and unique identifier of the user or organization a resource belongs to.
For example, in the URL: https://acme.data.world/jonloyens/an-intro-to-dataworld-dataset, jonloyens is the unique identifier of the owner.

type.description: Filter by type of metadata resource. Both IRI and label are accepted.

updatedRange.description: Filter by range of date that the resource was updated.

internalError.description: Is the cause of a failure likely an internal bug in our code, not something users have control over?

serverDescription.description: Technical string describing the location of the database on the network.  This may be a JDBC url.

sslCertificateChain.description: If the ping failed due to an SSL issue

sslCertificateNameProblem.description: Did the ping fail because the server's name didn't match the name in the certificate?

sslCertificateTrustPathProblem.description: Did the ping fail because it didn't trust the server's certificate due to a lack of a path to a root CA?

sslNotSupported.description: Did the ping fail because it tried to use SSL but the server+port combination doesn't support SSL?

properties.description: All properties of the resource that are associated with a metadata presentation. Key is the IRI of the property. Value is the value of the property.

similarResources.description: Similar projects with the same owner

label.description: Label of the metadata presentation associated with the property

commentid.description: Comment id generated while creating a comment. Returned for reference but must be provided through URL only.

owner.description: An organization owning a comment. Returned for reference but must be provided through URL only.

topicUuid.description: Topic unique id stored along comments. Returned for reference but must be provided through URL only.

owner.description: ID of an organization that owns the resource
owner.title: organization id

owner.description: ID of an organization that owns the resource
owner.title: organization id

approverParty.description: The organization that can approve the request. Ignored in create request.

contribution.description: Provides details on the contribution, if this is a contribution request.

granteeOwner.description: If this is a transfer request, this is the organization that will receive the resource. Otherwise, this is the same as approverParty.

requestId.description: A unique id of the request assigned by the service and returned in response thus must not be provided in create request.

requesterParty.description: The user that created this request. Ignored in create request.

resource.description: The resource that the request is created for. Only DATASET and METADATA_RESOURCE are allowed.

transferAsset.description: Provides details on the transfer, if this is a transfer request.

defaultTopic.description: True if this is the default topic for the specified resource. There should only be 1 topic with this field set per resource. Must be provided while creating a topic only.

resource.description: A resource for which the topic is created.

topicId.description: Short friendly form of a topic id that uniquely identifies the topic within a resource.

topicSlug.description: Short friendly form of a topic title.

topicUuid.description: Unique id of the topic generated while create new one. Returned for reference but must be provided through URL only.

visibility.description: Topic visibility. The field is required.

endDate.description: Inclusive. Format is YYYY-MM-DD in UTC. If inputted by itself (without startDate), it will match all dates before this date and this date.

startDate.description: Inclusive. Format is YYYY-MM-DD in UTC. If inputted by itself (without endDate),it will match all dates after this date and this date.

requestid.description: ID of the request to reject.

sourceDatasetId.description: If source is a table or column, populate with database ID that contains table.

sourceTableId.description: If source is a column, populate with table ID that contains column.

sourceTypeIri.description: IRI of specified sourceType, this is required when sourceType is generic.

targetDatasetId.description: If target is a table or column, populate with database ID that contains table.

targetTableId.description: If target is a column, populate with table ID that contains column.

byRelationTypes.description: Filter by these relation types. Relation types should be IRIs.
Filter by these relation types. Relation types should be IRIs.
Filter by these relation types. Relation types are identified by their IRI.

byResourceTypes.description: Filter by these resource types. Resource types are identified by their IRI.

labels.description: Label of the relationship presentation associated with the relationship

hierarchy.description: Parent types of this resource's type. Returns the most specific type followed by its parent. The first item is the same as type. The last item is the root type.

owner.description: The ID of the organization that this resource belongs to

type.description: Resource simple type name, e.g. catalogDatabase, catalogEntry etc.

createdRange.description: Filter by range of date that the resource was created.

minAccessLevel.description: Minimum access level to filter by.

owner.description: Filter by owners. Owners are identified by their IDs.

type.description: Filter by type of metadata resource. Both IRI and label are accepted.

updatedRange.description: Filter by range of date that the resource was updated.

desiredUsername.description: Used to create service account user name

displayName.description: Display name to show in UI

active.description: Indicate service account token enabled or disabled

enum: Public Domain, PDDL, CC-0, CC-BY, CDLA-Permissive-1.0, CC-BY-IGO, CC-BY 3.0, CC-BY 3.0 AU, CC-BY 3.0 IGO, CC-BY-SA, CC-BY-SA 3.0, CDLA-Sharing-1.0, CC BY-NC, CC BY-ND, CC BY-ND 3.0, CC-BY 3.0 NZ, CC-BY-NC 3.0, CC BY-NC-ND, CC-BY-NC-SA 3.0, CC-BY-SA 3.0 NZ, CC-BY-NC-SA 3.0 NZ, CC-BY-NC 3.0 NZ, CC-BY-NC-ND-NZ-3.0, CC BY-NC-SA, Italian-ODL, MIT License, OGL, OGL-Canada, OGL-Nova Scotia, OGL-UK, OSODL, ODC-BY, ODC-ODbL, Other

owner.description: Filter by owners. Owners are identified by their IDs.

entityType.description: The type of resource being transferred

identifier.description: The dct:identifier of resource being transferred

iri.description: The IRI of resource being transferred

groups.description: Groups in the organization that user should be assigned to

orgAgentId.description: Organization that user should be assigned to
